project.name: SimaOps AI
project.description: Enterprise-grade, cloud-portable operations platform for Sima Arome, an Indonesian natural-extracts manufacturer. Unifies lot intake, AI-assisted QC, human QC approval, cold-chain warehouse assignment, and production-readiness dashboards into a single auditable system.

nodes.name: main.go
nodes.summary: Entry point for the SimaOps Connect-RPC API: loads config, opens the DB pool, initializes OpenTelemetry, builds the events hub and NATS subscriber, wires JWT/RBAC/audit/idempotency middleware around the Connect and SSE handlers, and starts background workers.
nodes.languageNotes: Go main wiring graceful shutdown via context cancellation and background goroutines for periodic workers.

nodes.name: jwt.go
nodes.summary: JWT authentication middleware that fetches Keycloak JWKS, validates bearer tokens, extracts claims (sub, roles, exp) into the request context, and classifies token errors for SSE clients.
nodes.languageNotes: Lazy JWKS initialization guarded by a mutex; context keys carry parsed claims downstream.

nodes.name: jwt_test.go
nodes.summary: Unit test covering classifyTokenError mapping of JWT validation errors to client-facing reason codes.

nodes.name: keycloak_admin.go

nodes.name: rbac.go
nodes.summary: Role-based access control middleware mapping request paths to required roles and rejecting requests whose token roles do not satisfy them.

nodes.name: envelope.go
nodes.summary: Defines the canonical event Envelope (event id, type, actor, owner, resource, payload) plus constructors for creating and parsing envelopes carried over NATS and SSE.

nodes.name: envelope_test.go
nodes.summary: Unit tests validating envelope construction shape, nil/map payload handling, invalid JSON parsing, and event id uniqueness.

nodes.name: filter.go
nodes.summary: Authorization filter for the events stream: computes allowed subject patterns per role and decides whether a given envelope may be delivered to a subscriber based on role, ownership, and subject matching.

nodes.summary: Package root for the warehouse-intelligence service that wires the smart-slotting, hazard-segregation, and cold-chain engines into a unified WarehouseIntelligenceSystem facade plus a thin API adapter.
nodes.languageNotes: Python package barrel that composes domain engines behind a single orchestration class.

nodes.name: api.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI application exposing warehouse optimization over HTTP: a slotting recommendation endpoint, a cold-chain status endpoint, and a health check, with a background sensor simulation loop started on app lifespan.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses FastAPI lifespan asynccontextmanager to spawn an asyncio background sensor loop; Pydantic models define request/response schemas.

nodes.name: cold_chain_monitoring.py
nodes.summary: Cold-chain monitoring engine combining rule-based threshold checks, trend analysis, and an LSTM temperature-forecasting model to detect and predict refrigeration violations and emit graded alerts.
nodes.languageNotes: Defines a Keras LSTM model alongside dataclass/enum domain types; degrades gracefully when ML libraries are unavailable.

nodes.name: hazard_segregation.py
nodes.summary: Hazard segregation compliance engine that validates item and drum placements against classification, adjacency, distance, temperature, ventilation, and emergency-access rules, producing auditable compliance results.
nodes.languageNotes: Rule checks accumulate violations/warnings/recommendations into a ComplianceResult dataclass with an audit trail.

nodes.name: smart_slotting.py
nodes.summary: Smart slotting engine that mines frequent itemsets from transactions (via mlxtend FP-growth or a custom FP-tree fallback), derives association rules, and converts them into rack placement recommendations.

nodes.name: WarehouseIntelligenceSystem

nodes.name: WarehouseIntelligenceAPI

nodes.languageNotes: Builds a stacked LSTM with dropout via keras.Sequential; falls back to synthetic predictions when no trained model is present.

nodes.name: ColdChainMonitoringEngine
nodes.summary: Stateful monitoring engine that validates sensor readings, applies per-equipment thresholds, runs LSTM-based violation prediction and trend analysis, raises graded alerts, and computes equipment health scores.

nodes.name: HazardSegregationEngine
nodes.summary: Compliance engine validating drum and item placements through layered checks (classification, location compatibility, temperature, adjacency, segregation distance, ventilation, emergency access) and maintaining an exportable audit trail.

nodes.summary: Demonstration routine exercising the hazard engine with safe, prohibited-adjacency, and temperature-violation placement scenarios and printing compliance reports.

nodes.name: FPNode

nodes.name: SmartSlottingEngine
nodes.summary: Slotting engine that loads transactions, mines frequent itemsets (mlxtend or custom FP-growth), generates association rules, assigns racks, and produces slotting recommendations through a full pipeline.

nodes.name: example_smart_slotting
nodes.summary: Demonstration routine that builds sample transactions, runs the full slotting pipeline, and prints discovered rules and rack recommendations.

nodes.name: TestHazardSegregation

nodes.name: TestColdChainMonitoring

nodes.name: TestSystemIntegration

nodes.name: auth.js
nodes.summary: Keycloak OAuth token management for the k6 load-test harness, handling password and refresh grants, per-VU token caching with leeway-based expiry, and role-based token getters for operator, supervisor, warehouse, manager, and admin users.
nodes.languageNotes: k6 script using custom Trend/Counter metrics and a per-VU in-memory token cache keyed by username to minimize Keycloak grant traffic during load runs.

nodes.name: helpers.js
nodes.summary: Random test-data generation utilities for the k6 pipeline, providing UUID generation, near-future date strings, randomized lot payloads, QC image byte stubs, and array sampling.

nodes.name: pipeline.js
nodes.summary: Drives the full lot to QC to dispatch operations pipeline against the SimaOps API during load tests, issuing authenticated RPC calls across multiple stages and recording custom completion, duration, and AI-processing metrics.
nodes.languageNotes: k6 script that sequences a multi-step business workflow with idempotency keys and per-stage check() assertions, emitting custom Trend/Counter metrics for each stage outcome.

nodes.name: smoke.js
nodes.summary: Smoke-test scenario entry point that runs the lot-to-dispatch pipeline at minimal VU load to verify the system is functional end to end before heavier runs.

nodes.name: soak.js
nodes.summary: Soak-test scenario entry point that sustains steady pipeline load over an extended duration to surface memory leaks, resource exhaustion, and degradation under prolonged stress.

nodes.name: validation.js

nodes.name: postToken
nodes.summary: Posts a form-encoded token request to the Keycloak token endpoint and records the request against a tagged auth counter.

nodes.name: passwordGrant
nodes.summary: Performs an OAuth password grant for a given username with jittered sleep, validates the response, and returns the parsed token, recording grant and failure metrics.

nodes.name: refreshGrant
nodes.summary: Performs an OAuth refresh-token grant to renew an access token, validating the response and recording refresh and failure metrics before returning the parsed token.

nodes.name: setupAuth
nodes.summary: k6 setup hook that pre-warms authentication by acquiring an initial token before the load test begins.

nodes.name: tokenFor
nodes.summary: Returns a valid access token for a username, serving cached tokens when fresh and otherwise refreshing or re-acquiring via password grant, with per-VU expiry leeway.

nodes.name: uuid
nodes.summary: Generates a v4-style UUID string from random bytes for use as unique identifiers and idempotency keys in test payloads.

nodes.name: randomLotData

nodes.name: rpc
nodes.summary: Issues an authenticated JSON RPC POST to the API with optional idempotency key and tagged metrics, returning the response and safely parsed body.

nodes.name: runPipeline
nodes.summary: Executes the complete lot to QC to dispatch workflow as a sequence of authenticated RPC and upload calls across roles, asserting each stage and recording completion, AI-processing, and total-duration metrics.

nodes.name: iter
nodes.summary: Per-VU iteration body that seeds the token cache from setup data, runs the full pipeline, and applies randomized think-time between iterations.

nodes.name: clock_test.go
nodes.summary: Unit tests for Keycloak clock-skew detection, covering the happy path, an offset Date header, hysteresis-based readiness gating, and graceful tolerance when Keycloak is unreachable.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses httptest.NewServer to stub the Keycloak Date response and t.Setenv/os.Setenv to drive environment-based configuration.

nodes.name: health.go
nodes.summary: HTTP liveness and readiness handlers; the readiness check pings the database and MinIO and evaluates Keycloak clock skew before reporting ready.

nodes.name: health_test.go
nodes.summary: Smoke tests for the liveness (Healthz) and readiness (Readyz) HTTP handlers using httptest request/recorder pairs.

nodes.name: warehouse_slot_test.go
nodes.summary: Table-driven tests for the drum-to-slot acceptance rule, verifying hazard-class allowances and drum-compatibility matching against JSON-array slot attributes.

nodes.name: minio.go
nodes.summary: MinIO/S3 object-storage client wrapper providing presigned PUT/GET URL generation and a connectivity Ping used by the readiness probe.
nodes.languageNotes: Wraps the minio-go SDK; configuration is pulled from environment variables in NewMinIOClient.

nodes.name: TestCheckKeycloakClockSkew_HappyPath
nodes.summary: Asserts CheckKeycloakClockSkew returns no error when the stubbed Keycloak server reports a current Date header.

nodes.name: TestCheckKeycloakClockSkew_OffsetDate
nodes.summary: Verifies CheckKeycloakClockSkew detects a 90-second skew when the server returns a back-dated Date header.

nodes.name: TestCheckSkewForReadiness_HysteresisAndPass
nodes.summary: Exercises the hysteresis logic of CheckSkewForReadiness, confirming the failure counter accumulates on skew and resets once the clock is back in tolerance.

nodes.name: TestCheckSkewForReadiness_KeycloakUnreachable_DoesNotFail
nodes.summary: Ensures readiness does not fail when Keycloak is unreachable, treating connectivity errors as non-fatal to the skew check.

nodes.name: Healthz
nodes.summary: Liveness handler that always returns 200 with a JSON status body.

nodes.name: ReadyzHandler
nodes.summary: Factory returning a readiness handler that pings the database and MinIO and checks Keycloak clock skew, reporting 503 with a reason when any dependency is unhealthy.

nodes.name: Readyz
nodes.summary: Lightweight readiness handler variant returning a static ready JSON response.

nodes.name: TestHealthz
nodes.summary: Verifies the Healthz handler returns 200 and the expected JSON content-type header.

nodes.name: TestReadyz
nodes.summary: Verifies the Readyz handler responds successfully to a readiness request.

nodes.name: slotAcceptsDrum
nodes.summary: Test helper replicating slot acceptance logic: a slot accepts a drum when hazard class is allowed and the drum's compatibility codes match the slot's JSON-array attributes.

nodes.name: TestSlotAcceptsDrum
nodes.summary: Table-driven test covering hazard-allowed and drum-compatibility permutations for the slotAcceptsDrum rule.

nodes.name: MinIOClient
nodes.summary: Struct wrapping a minio-go client, exposing presigned URL generation and a connectivity Ping.

nodes.name: NewMinIOClient
nodes.summary: Constructs a MinIOClient from environment-derived endpoint, credentials, and TLS settings, returning an error on initialization failure.

nodes.name: PresignedPutURL
nodes.summary: Generates a presigned PUT URL for uploading an object with a given content type and expiry.

nodes.name: PresignedGetURL
nodes.summary: Generates a presigned GET URL for downloading an object with a given expiry.

nodes.name: Ping
nodes.summary: Connectivity check that lists buckets to confirm MinIO reachability, used by the readiness probe.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Single-stage container image for the Python AI inference worker, built on python:3.12-slim, installing the package's inference extras and exposing port 8081 to serve QC inference and health endpoints.
nodes.languageNotes: Sets MODEL_PATH=models/best.pt at build time and launches via CMD ["python", "main.py"].

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Multi-stage build for the Go Connect API: compiles a static CGO-disabled binary in golang:1.26-alpine, then ships it in a distroless nonroot image exposing port 8080.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses gcr.io/distroless/static-debian12:nonroot for a minimal, rootless runtime; builds from ./cmd/api with -ldflags="-s -w" to strip the binary.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Multi-stage build for the Go outbox-to-NATS publisher: compiles a static binary in golang:1.26-alpine, then runs it in a distroless nonroot image with no exposed ports.
nodes.languageNotes: Builds from ./cmd/ into /publisher and runs as a headless worker (ENTRYPOINT only, no EXPOSE) on the distroless nonroot base.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Hardened multi-stage build for the Python warehouse-intelligence service: installs pip dependencies in a python:3.11-slim builder, then copies them into a slim runtime that runs gunicorn as a non-root user on port 8000 with a configured HEALTHCHECK.
nodes.languageNotes: Security-hardened: creates an unprivileged 'warehouse' user (uid 1000), uses --chown on COPY, installs deps with pip --user into /home/warehouse/.local, and defines a HEALTHCHECK probe before launching gunicorn.

nodes.name: .dockerignore
nodes.summary: Docker build-context exclusion list for the SvelteKit web BFF, keeping local artifacts like node_modules and build output out of the image build context.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Multi-stage build for the SvelteKit web BFF: installs deps and runs vite build in a node:20-alpine builder, then serves the adapter-node output via 'node build' on port 3000.
nodes.languageNotes: Runs 'npx svelte-kit sync && npx vite build' in the builder stage and copies build/, package.json, and node_modules into the runtime image; sets PORT=3000 and launches the adapter-node server with CMD ["node", "build"].

nodes.name: build.yaml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions CI workflow that runs per-service test gates (Go vet/test for api and outbox-publisher, pytest for ai-worker and warehouse-intelligence, svelte-check/vitest for web), then builds and pushes Docker images for all five SimaOps services to GHCR. Triggers on push and pull_request to main.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a matrix strategy over five apps for the build job, gated by `needs` on every test job so a failing test blocks docker push; images are pushed only on push events (not PRs).

nodes.name: deploy-production.yaml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow that deploys all five SimaOps services to the production OKE (Oracle Kubernetes Engine) cluster via Helm upgrade --install, tagging images by the release tag. Triggers on GitHub release publication under the protected `production` environment.
nodes.languageNotes: Release-gated production deploy using OCI CLI secrets and azure/setup-helm; iterates apps in a shell loop with a 10m Helm wait timeout.

nodes.name: deploy-staging.yaml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow that deploys all five SimaOps services to the staging OKE cluster via Helm after the Build & Push workflow completes successfully on main, tagging images by the build commit SHA.
nodes.languageNotes: Chained via `workflow_run` on the Build & Push workflow with a conclusion=='success' guard; deploys SHA-tagged images with a 5m Helm wait timeout.

nodes.name: lint.yaml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow that sets up Go, Node, and pnpm toolchains and runs `make lint` across the monorepo. Triggers on push and pull_request to main.
nodes.languageNotes: Delegates all linting to the repository Makefile's `lint` target after provisioning the required language runtimes.

nodes.name: roles
nodes.summary: Reference table of RBAC roles (id, name, description) used to gate access across the operations platform.

nodes.name: users_profile
nodes.summary: User account profiles with username, email, full_name, active flag, and audit timestamps.

nodes.name: user_roles
nodes.summary: Join table mapping users to roles, implementing the many-to-many RBAC assignment between users_profile and roles.

nodes.name: lots
nodes.summary: Core inventory lots table tracking incoming material (supplier, material type, quantity, storage requirement) and its lifecycle status; the central entity the QC, warehouse, and dispatch flows hang off.

nodes.name: qc_jobs
nodes.summary: Quality-control job records tracking an image-based QC request per lot, including status FSM, requester, failure reason, and start/complete timestamps.

nodes.name: qc_results
nodes.summary: AI QC outcomes per job: recommendation, confidence, findings JSON, model version, annotated image key, and a supervisor review/decision trail.

nodes.name: warehouse_locations
nodes.summary: Warehouse storage slots with zone, temperature range, hazard/drum compatibility constraints, capacity, and current status for placement decisions.

nodes.name: warehouse_assignments
nodes.summary: Assignment records placing a lot into a warehouse location, capturing who assigned it, when, and assignment status.

nodes.name: audit_logs
nodes.summary: Immutable audit trail capturing actor, action, entity type/id, before/after JSON snapshots, and request/trace correlation IDs for every mutating operation.

nodes.name: outbox_events
nodes.summary: Transactional outbox table storing event_type, payload JSON, status, and retry count for reliable event publishing to NATS via the outbox pattern.

nodes.name: idempotency_keys
nodes.summary: Stores cached responses keyed by user_id + operation so repeated mutating requests are deduplicated and return the original result.

nodes.name: 002_outbox_publishing_state.sql
nodes.summary: Widens outbox_events.status ENUM to add a PUBLISHING intermediate state, letting the publisher atomically claim a batch (PENDING to PUBLISHING) before pushing to NATS; instant DDL in TiDB.
nodes.languageNotes: ENUM widening is an instant, lock-free DDL in TiDB, safe to apply on a live cluster.

nodes.name: dispatches
nodes.summary: Adds the dispatches table tracking a production-ready lot leaving the facility, with its own lifecycle FSM (PENDING to SCHEDULED to IN_TRANSIT to DELIVERED, plus CANCELLED) and a FK to lots; closes the intake to dispatch loop.

nodes.name: 004_composite_indexes.sql
nodes.summary: Index-tuning migration adding (filter_col, created_at) composite and covering indexes across qc_results, lots, qc_jobs, dispatches, audit_logs, and warehouse_assignments to eliminate full scans and in-memory TopN sorts, then dropping the now-redundant single-column indexes.
nodes.languageNotes: ADD/DROP INDEX use IF [NOT] EXISTS for idempotency; ADD INDEX is an online, lock-free operation in TiDB.

nodes.name: design.md
nodes.summary: Authoritative architecture spec for SimaOps AI, detailing the event-driven system: SvelteKit BFF, Go connect-go API, Python ONNX AI worker, leader-elected outbox publisher, TiDB, MinIO, NATS JetStream, and Keycloak. Covers the repository layout, six Connect RPC services, lot/QC workflow state machine, key domain tables, the config-driven AI findings map, Helm/SST deployment matrix, and the plan-v5 realtime SSE architecture with standardized event envelope and three-tier auth recovery.
nodes.languageNotes: Kiro design spec with Mermaid architecture diagram, ASCII state-machine and SSE flow diagrams, and Go code snippets for the role+owner event filter.

nodes.name: requirements.md
nodes.summary: Functional and non-functional requirements spec for SimaOps AI, a single enterprise platform for Sima Arome's natural-extracts operations (intake to AI triage to human QC to warehouse to production handoff). Locks the tech stack, hard constraints (SST, OKE/OCI, Connect RPC, no cloud lock-in, BFF auth, AI assistive-only), RBAC roles, domain reality (material classes, cold-chain, IBC/IPPC drums), reliability and security requirements, and the plan-v5 realtime UI requirements (R-RT-1 through R-RT-7).
nodes.languageNotes: Kiro requirements spec with hackathon focus-area coverage matrix, locked-decision Q&A table, and versioned approval history.

nodes.name: tasks.md
nodes.summary: Implementation task breakdown for SimaOps AI across five phases (Foundation, Core workflow, Enterprise reliability, Deployment, AI polish) covering 30 build tasks plus the completed 23-task plan-v5 realtime web UI initiative. Each task names concrete deliverables: Protobuf/Buf codegen, TiDB+Atlas+sqlc, SvelteKit shell, Lot/QC/Warehouse/Audit/Dashboard services, Keycloak BFF auth, idempotency, outbox, NATS AI worker, OpenTelemetry, Helm/SST/OKE deployment, and ONNX inference.
nodes.languageNotes: Kiro tasks spec organized by phase with per-task deliverable bullets, phase-summary and execution-status tables, and a plan-v5 completion checklist.

nodes.name: Makefile
nodes.summary: Root developer-experience orchestration with phony targets for proto codegen (buf), multi-language linting, the local docker-compose stack lifecycle, Atlas/sqlc database tasks, Helm linting, and Playwright demo flows. Self-documenting via a help target that greps target docstrings.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses .PHONY declarations and an awk/grep self-documenting help target that parses '## ' comments from MAKEFILE_LIST; several targets are stubbed placeholders (echo TODO).

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project overview for SimaOps AI describing the SvelteKit BFF → Go API → TiDB architecture, the AI worker and NATS event pipeline, local-dev quickstart, project layout, and a detailed Server-Sent Events realtime section covering event envelope schema, NATS subjects, RBAC/owner filtering, and auth-refresh resilience tiers.

nodes.name: buf.gen.yaml

nodes.languageNotes: Uses buf managed-mode override for go_package_prefix and remote plugins (buf.build/...) for multi-language generation, including protobuf-es v2 which bundles Connect-ES service descriptors.

nodes.name: buf.yaml
nodes.summary: Buf v2 module configuration declaring the proto module (buf.build/simaops/api), STANDARD lint rules (excluding PACKAGE_VERSION_SUFFIX), and FILE-level breaking-change detection for the protobuf API surface.

nodes.name: docker-compose.yml

nodes.name: tidb
nodes.summary: TiDB v7.5.0 distributed MySQL-compatible SQL database exposed on port 4000 (MySQL protocol) and 10080 (status/metrics), health-checked via mysqladmin ping.

nodes.name: minio
nodes.summary: MinIO S3-compatible object storage serving the API on 9000 and a console on 9001, with a named data volume and root credentials for local development.

nodes.name: minio-init
nodes.summary: One-shot mc client container that waits for MinIO health then creates the QC images, QC results, reports, and model-artifacts buckets.

nodes.name: nats

nodes.name: keycloak

nodes.name: otel-collector

nodes.name: jaeger

nodes.name: package.json

nodes.summary: pnpm workspace manifest enrolling the apps/web SvelteKit frontend and the infra/sst deployment package into the monorepo.

nodes.name: skills-lock.json
nodes.summary: Lockfile pinning an installed agent skill (impeccable, sourced from the pbakaus/impeccable GitHub repo) by path and content hash for reproducible tooling.

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Local development environment template for the SimaOps Go API, declaring runtime settings for the HTTP port plus connections to TiDB, MinIO object storage, NATS messaging, Keycloak OIDC auth, and OpenTelemetry tracing.
nodes.languageNotes: TIDB_DSN uses the go-sql-driver/mysql DSN format with parseTime=true; secrets here are dev-only placeholders and must be overridden in production.

nodes.name: go.mod
nodes.summary: Go module manifest for the SimaOps API (module github.com/taling-dev/CYBERHACK-2026/apps/api) on Go 1.26.1, declaring direct dependencies including connectrpc, the MySQL driver, JWT/keyfunc auth, MinIO, NATS, Prometheus, and OpenTelemetry.
nodes.languageNotes: Direct requires (connectrpc, go-sql-driver/mysql, minio-go, nats.go, prometheus client, otel) are separated from transitive // indirect dependencies across distinct require blocks.

nodes.name: go.sum
nodes.summary: Cryptographic checksum lockfile pinning the exact module versions and hashes for every direct and transitive dependency of the SimaOps API, ensuring reproducible and tamper-resistant Go builds.
nodes.languageNotes: Each module appears with both an h1: module hash and a /go.mod hash for module-graph verification by the Go toolchain.

nodes.name: sqlc.yaml
nodes.summary: sqlc v2 code-generation config that compiles SQL in internal/db/queries against the MySQL schema in db/migrations to produce type-safe Go data-access code (package db) under internal/db, emitting JSON tags and a querier interface.
nodes.languageNotes: engine: mysql with emit_interface and emit_json_tags enabled; prepared queries disabled. schema points outside the app to the shared ../../db/migrations directory.

nodes.name: audit.sql
nodes.summary: sqlc query group for the append-only audit_logs table: inserts audit entries with actor/action/entity context plus request and trace IDs, and lists logs globally, by entity, or by actor with pagination and a total count. Generates the audit.sql.go data layer.
nodes.languageNotes: Read queries COALESCE before_json/after_json to JSON_OBJECT() so null change payloads surface as empty objects rather than NULL.

nodes.name: dispatch.sql
nodes.summary: sqlc query group for the dispatches table: creates dispatches (default PENDING), fetches by id with a FOR UPDATE pessimistic lock, counts active (non-cancelled) dispatches per lot, lists/counts by status or lot, and updates status. Backs the dispatch state machine and TOCTOU guards.
nodes.languageNotes: GetDispatchForUpdate uses SELECT ... FOR UPDATE (TiDB pessimistic mode) to serialize concurrent status transitions against the FSM check.

nodes.name: lots.sql
nodes.summary: sqlc query group for the lots table: creates material lots (default DRAFT), fetches by id (with FOR UPDATE locking) or lot_number, lists/counts by status or material type, updates status, and aggregates lot counts grouped by status. Drives the lot lifecycle FSM.
nodes.languageNotes: GetLotForUpdate locks the row to close a read-then-write TOCTOU race on the status column; CountLotsByStatusGroup is a GROUP BY status aggregation.

nodes.name: outbox.sql
nodes.summary: sqlc query group implementing the transactional outbox over outbox_events plus idempotency_keys: claims PENDING batches into PUBLISHING, reads claimed rows, marks published/failed, releases or recovers stuck events, and manages idempotency-key create/read/update with 24h expiry cleanup. Enables singleton-leader, at-least-once event publishing.
nodes.languageNotes: ClaimOutboxBatch uses UPDATE ... WHERE status='PENDING' ORDER BY created_at ASC LIMIT ? for atomic batch claiming; ResetStuckPublishingEvents recovers events orphaned by a crashed leader, relying on NATS Nats-Msg-Id dedup for exactly-once delivery downstream.

nodes.name: qc.sql
nodes.summary: sqlc query group spanning qc_jobs and qc_results: enqueues QC jobs (default QUEUED), guards duplicates via active-job counts (with same-image detection), supersedes/requeues jobs, drives the QC lifecycle status transitions, stores AI results and supervisor review decisions, and aggregates recommendations and average confidence over a time window.
nodes.languageNotes: Active-job detection relies on status IN ('QUEUED','PROCESSING','AI_COMPLETED','NEEDS_HUMAN_REVIEW'); FAILED is treated as terminal and excluded from dashboard recommendation metrics.

nodes.name: users.sql
nodes.summary: sqlc query group for RBAC over users_profile, roles, and user_roles: lists users with comma-joined role names, fetches users by username/id, lists/fetches roles, and assigns/revokes user-role mappings idempotently. Backs authentication and authorization lookups.
nodes.languageNotes: ListUsers uses GROUP_CONCAT(r.name SEPARATOR ',') over LEFT JOINed roles; AssignUserRole uses INSERT IGNORE for idempotent role grants.

nodes.name: warehouse.sql
nodes.summary: sqlc query group over warehouse_locations and warehouse_assignments: lists/filters locations by zone or availability, performs atomic capacity increment/decrement for slot allocation, creates and fetches lot assignments, and computes per-zone total/occupied/available capacity metrics via a LEFT JOIN aggregation.
nodes.languageNotes: DecrementLocationCapacityAtomic uses :execrows with a guarded UPDATE (capacity > 0) so the caller can detect contention; ZoneCapacityMetrics derives occupancy from a correlated LEFT JOIN counting ACTIVE assignments.

The HPA scales 2-6 replicas on 60% CPU utilization (memory intentionally omitted), with fast scale-up and conservative 600s scale-down windows tuned for long-lived SSE connections; the PDB enforces minAvailable=1 for zero-downtime node drains and rolling upgrades.

Bundles a 50Gi oci-bv PersistentVolumeClaim, a single-replica Recreate-strategy Deployment mounting /data with root credentials from a Secret, a ClusterIP Service exposing port 9000 (S3 API) and 9001 (console), and an idempotent bucket-init Job that provisions the four simaops-* buckets once MinIO is ready.
nodes.languageNotes: Four-document manifest (PVC + Deployment + Service + Job). Uses Recreate strategy because the RWO volume cannot be shared across pods, securityContext uid/gid 1000 with fsGroup, liveness/readiness probes on /minio/health, and an `mc` mb bootstrap Job with backoffLimit and TTL cleanup.

Secured with cert-manager Let's Encrypt TLS, forced HTTPS redirect, and htpasswd basic-auth (Secret `prometheus-basic-auth`) so k6's remote_write client can push metrics.
nodes.languageNotes: Single networking.k8s.io/v1 Ingress with nginx auth-type/auth-secret basic-auth annotations and proxy-body-size/timeout tuning for remote-write payloads.

nodes.name: simaops-ingress.yaml

Routes host app.161.118.244.229.sslip.io path `/` to the simaops-web Service on port 3000 and host api.161.118.244.229.sslip.io path `/` to the simaops-api Service on port 8080, sharing the simaops-tls certificate.
nodes.languageNotes: Single Ingress with extensive nginx annotations: proxy-buffering off and 3600s read/send timeouts for chunked text/event-stream SSE, raised proxy-buffer-size (16k) to fit large OIDC session cookies, plus explicit CORS allow-credentials/headers/methods/origin and cert-manager letsencrypt-prod TLS.

nodes.name: chaos-ai-worker.sh
nodes.summary: Fault-injection chaos test that force-kills an AI worker pod mid-batch and asserts NATS pull-consumer redelivery recovers every un-acked message: no duplicate qc_results, no qc_jobs stuck in PROCESSING, and HPA brings the pod back.
nodes.languageNotes: Runs throwaway mysql/nats-box pods via kubectl run for DB and JetStream queries; uses a background k6 process (K6_PID) plus a timed recovery loop around the 120s ack_wait window.

nodes.name: dbq
nodes.summary: Helper that spins up a throwaway mysql:8.0 pod via kubectl run to execute a SELECT COUNT(*) against TiDB, filtering out pod-status noise and returning the first integer line.

nodes.name: chaos-nats.sh
nodes.summary: Fault-injection chaos test that scales the NATS StatefulSet to 0 for ~30s and asserts the outbox pattern keeps the API accepting writes, the publisher logs release-to-PENDING, and the backlog drains within 60s with no new FAILED rows once NATS returns.
nodes.languageNotes: Installs an EXIT trap (restore_nats) so NATS is always scaled back to 1 replica even on failure or Ctrl-C; probes /readyz throughout the outage window.

nodes.name: chaos-outbox-publisher.sh
nodes.summary: Fault-injection chaos test that force-deletes the outbox-publisher leader pod mid-claim and asserts the B-07 reset-on-startup path: lease handoff completes, the new leader resets stuck PUBLISHING rows to PENDING, and the outbox drains with no new FAILED rows.
nodes.languageNotes: Polls for a new leader pod distinct from the killed one, then greps the new leader's logs for the became-leader / reset-on-startup recovery message.

nodes.name: postflight.sh
nodes.summary: Post-run cluster sanity reporter that emits a markdown report (pod and HPA state, scaling events, lots/outbox DB breakdowns, NATS/JetStream lag) and computes a single PASS/FAIL verdict from the k6 exit code, container restarts, and lots stuck in transient states.
nodes.languageNotes: Takes scenario/run-start/k6-exit as positional args; all data-collection sections are best-effort (never exit 1) while the verdict section accumulates a PROBLEMS counter.

nodes.name: preflight.sh
nodes.summary: Hard prerequisite gate run before any load test: verifies kubectl context, pod readiness, ingress endpoints, MinIO buckets, demo-user auth, metrics-server, and HPA scaling-active, then snapshots baseline replicas and safely resets warehouse_locations capacity behind a seed-signature assertion.
nodes.languageNotes: Exits non-zero on any failed check so runners abort early; guards the capacity reset with a bash assertion that warehouse_locations contains exactly the 12 seeded codes before overwriting.

nodes.name: run-smoke.sh
nodes.summary: Smoke-run orchestrator (1 VU x 1 min): runs preflight, invokes the k6 smoke scenario with optional Prometheus remote-write, then runs postflight and assembles a markdown report, propagating the k6 exit code.
nodes.languageNotes: Captures the piped k6 exit code via PIPESTATUS while tee-ing stdout to the reports directory; sources ~/.k6/prom.env when present to enable remote-write.

nodes.name: run-soak.sh
nodes.summary: Soak-run orchestrator (5 VUs x 2 hr): runs preflight, invokes the k6 soak scenario, holds a 5-minute idle drain to confirm metrics return to baseline, then runs postflight and writes a markdown report.
nodes.languageNotes: Mirrors run-smoke but adds a 300s post-run idle window before postflight so steady-state recovery is captured.

nodes.name: run-validation.sh
nodes.summary: Validation-run orchestrator (20 VUs x 22 min): runs preflight, invokes the k6 validation scenario, holds a 10-minute window to observe HPA scale-down, then runs postflight and writes a markdown report.
nodes.languageNotes: Adds a 600s post-run hold for HPA scale-down stabilization before postflight; propagates the k6 exit code as the script's exit status.

nodes.name: e2e-realtime.sh
nodes.summary: End-to-end realtime acceptance test that authenticates five role-based users, opens five concurrent SSE streams against /events, runs the full lot to QC to warehouse to dispatch pipeline, and asserts each role only receives the event subjects its permissions allow.
nodes.languageNotes: Bash wrapper that execs an inline Python3 heredoc using urllib and threading to drive concurrent SSE clients and a role-based event-filter expectations matrix.

nodes.name: e2e-token-refresh.sh
nodes.summary: End-to-end auth-resilience test that temporarily shortens the Keycloak realm accessTokenLifespan, holds an SSE stream open across token expiry with a reconnect/refresh loop, and asserts the client observes multiple connection-info frames with strictly-advancing token expiry before restoring the original realm setting.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a bash EXIT trap (cleanup) to guarantee realm restoration, then execs an inline Python3 reconnect loop that refreshes tokens on HTTP 401 mid-stream.

nodes.name: wait-for-stack.sh

nodes.summary: Abstract quality-control strategy interface defining the async analyze contract implemented by the mock and classifier backends.

nodes.name: MockStrategy
nodes.summary: Mock QC strategy that returns canned analysis results after a short delay, used for local development and testing without a real model.

nodes.name: ClassifierStrategy
nodes.summary: Production QC strategy that lazily loads MinIO, the YOLO model, and the fuzzy system, downloads the image, runs classification, and grades quality via fuzzy inference.

nodes.name: _build_fuzzy_system
nodes.summary: Constructs the scikit-fuzzy control system (healthy/defect antecedents, quality consequent, membership functions, and rules) used to grade QC results.

nodes.name: _grade
nodes.summary: Runs a fuzzy control-system simulation on healthy and defect scores to compute a numeric quality grade.

nodes.name: get_strategy
nodes.summary: Factory that selects and instantiates the QC strategy (mock or classifier) based on the configured inference mode.

nodes.name: mark_job_processing
nodes.summary: Transitions a QC job row to the processing state in Postgres with commit/rollback handling.

nodes.name: write_qc_result
nodes.summary: Persists the QC inference result and recommendation for a job and lot to Postgres inside a transaction.

nodes.name: mark_job_failed
nodes.summary: Marks a QC job as failed in Postgres, recording the failure reason with commit/rollback handling.

nodes.name: fetch_lot_number

nodes.name: build_envelope
nodes.summary: Constructs a standardized event envelope (id, type, actor, owner, resource, payload, timestamp) and serializes it to JSON bytes for NATS publishing.

nodes.name: publish_envelope
nodes.summary: Publishes an event envelope to a NATS subject with OpenTelemetry trace-context injected into the message headers.

nodes.name: message_handler
nodes.summary: Core message processor: parses an incoming QC job envelope, marks the job processing, runs inference, writes results, emits result/failure events, and acks or naks the NATS message under a trace span.

nodes.name: run_consumer
nodes.summary: Connects to NATS JetStream, creates a pull subscription for QC-image events, and fetches/dispatches messages to the handler under a concurrency semaphore with reconnect and graceful-drain handling.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Process entry point that wires up logging, OpenTelemetry, the Postgres connection, the NATS/JetStream client and stream, signal-based shutdown, and starts the leader-elected publish loop.

nodes.name: runWithLeaderElection
nodes.summary: Runs the publisher under Kubernetes lease-based leader election so only the elected pod polls the outbox, falling back to a plain poll loop when running outside a cluster.

nodes.name: pollLoop
nodes.summary: Ticker-driven loop that, each cycle, updates the backlog gauge and publishes pending outbox events until the context is cancelled.

nodes.name: publishPending
nodes.summary: Claims a batch of PENDING outbox_events rows, publishes each to NATS JetStream with trace-context headers and dedup msg-id, and marks rows published or failed while recording metrics and spans.

nodes.name: initOTel
nodes.summary: Initializes the OpenTelemetry OTLP/gRPC trace exporter, tracer provider, and composite propagator, returning a shutdown function.

nodes.name: startHealthServer

nodes.summary: Maps an incoming realtime event subject to the set of TanStack Query keys to invalidate, refreshing only the affected lot, dispatch, coldchain, audit, and admin caches.

nodes.name: markSeen
nodes.summary: Records an event ID as seen in the localStorage-backed map (pruning expired entries) and returns whether it was already present, used to suppress duplicate toasts.

nodes.summary: Maps realtime event subjects to role-targeted toast specs and dispatches a toast only when the current user's roles match and the event has not already been seen.

nodes.name: auth.ts
nodes.summary: Server-side BFF auth core for Keycloak OIDC: PKCE code verifier/challenge generation, signed auth-state encoding, authorization/logout URL building, token exchange and resilient token refresh, JWT payload parsing, and the HttpOnly session cookie definitions.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Web Crypto (crypto.subtle / getRandomValues) and base64url helpers; refresh is single-flighted via an in-memory inflight map with retry/backoff and permanent-vs-transient RefreshError classification.

nodes.name: generateCodeVerifier
nodes.summary: Generates a cryptographically random PKCE code verifier using crypto.getRandomValues and base64url encoding.

nodes.name: generateCodeChallenge
nodes.summary: Derives the PKCE S256 code challenge from a verifier via a SHA-256 digest encoded as base64url.

nodes.name: decodeAuthState
nodes.summary: Decodes the base64url OIDC auth-state blob back into the structured state object, returning null on malformed input.

nodes.name: buildAuthorizationUrl
nodes.summary: Constructs the Keycloak authorization endpoint URL with client ID, redirect URI, scope, PKCE challenge, and encoded state for the OIDC login redirect.

nodes.name: buildLogoutUrl
nodes.summary: Builds the Keycloak end-session URL with the id_token_hint and post-logout redirect for OIDC logout.

nodes.name: exchangeCode
nodes.summary: Exchanges an OIDC authorization code plus PKCE verifier at the Keycloak token endpoint and returns the access/refresh/id token response.

nodes.name: refreshToken
nodes.summary: Performs a Keycloak refresh_token grant, classifying failures as permanent or transient RefreshError so callers can decide whether to retry or force re-login.

nodes.name: refreshTokenWithRetry
nodes.summary: Single-flights concurrent refreshes through an in-memory inflight map and retries transient failures with backoff while propagating permanent RefreshErrors immediately.

nodes.name: parseJwtPayload
nodes.summary: Decodes the payload segment of a JWT (base64url) into a JSON object without verifying the signature, used to read claims like roles and expiry.

nodes.name: RefreshError
nodes.summary: Typed error carrying a permanent/transient kind and reason so token-refresh callers can distinguish recoverable failures from ones requiring re-authentication.

nodes.name: guard.ts
nodes.summary: Route authorization guard that redirects unauthenticated users to login and throws 403 when the session lacks any of the allowed roles, used by protected page server loads.

nodes.name: requireRoles
nodes.summary: Asserts the request's user has at least one of the allowed roles, redirecting to login when anonymous and raising a 403 error otherwise.

nodes.name: simaops-realm.json

nodes.languageNotes: Security-relevant: sslRequired is 'none', the api client secret and all five user passwords are plaintext dev defaults — intended for local/seed use, not production.

nodes.name: go.work

nodes.summary: OpenTelemetry Collector configuration: OTLP gRPC/HTTP receivers, a batch processor, and traces/metrics/logs pipelines exporting traces to Jaeger (insecure) and everything to a debug exporter.

nodes.summary: Node package manifest for the @simaops/infra SST project, declaring deploy/diff/remove scripts and pinned SST 4.14.3 with the Pulumi OCI and Kubernetes providers.

nodes.name: sst-env.d.ts

nodes.languageNotes: Stage-aware (production retains/protects resources); worker cloud-init enforces chrony NTP sync before kubelet start to avoid JWT-validation failures from clock skew. Security list opens 22/80/443/6443 to 0.0.0.0/0 — SSH should be restricted in production.

nodes.summary: Operator guide for the k6 load-test harness covering prerequisites, setup, directory layout, how to run the smoke/validation/soak/chaos scenarios, naming conventions, and troubleshooting.

nodes.name: admin.proto
nodes.summary: Protobuf definition of AdminService (Connect RPC) with ListUsers, AssignRole, RevokeRole and ListRoles methods, the five-value Role enum, and User/RoleDefinition plus idempotent request/response messages for RBAC administration.
nodes.languageNotes: proto3 package simaops.admin.v1; role-mutation requests carry idempotency_key fields.

nodes.name: audit.proto
nodes.summary: Protobuf definition of AuditService (Connect RPC) with ListAuditLogs and GetEntityAuditTrail methods plus an AuditLog message capturing actor, action, entity, before/after JSON, request and trace IDs.
nodes.languageNotes: proto3 package simaops.audit.v1; imports google.protobuf.Timestamp for created_at.

nodes.name: dashboard.proto
nodes.summary: Protobuf definition of DashboardService (Connect RPC) with GetOpsDashboard, GetQCMetrics and GetWarehouseMetrics methods plus aggregate metric messages (status counts, QC pass rates, per-zone warehouse capacity).
nodes.languageNotes: proto3 package simaops.dashboard.v1; read-only reporting surface aggregating ops, QC and warehouse data.

nodes.name: dispatch.proto
nodes.summary: Protobuf definition of DispatchService (Connect RPC), the final stage of the operations pipeline: CreateDispatch, GetDispatch, ListDispatches and UpdateDispatchStatus methods, the DispatchStatus lifecycle enum, and Dispatch plus idempotent request/response messages.
nodes.languageNotes: proto3 package simaops.dispatch.v1; imports google.protobuf.Timestamp; mutation requests carry idempotency_key.

nodes.name: lot.proto
nodes.summary: Connect RPC schema for the LotService governing the lot intake lifecycle: create/get/list lots, status transitions, and timeline retrieval. Defines the core Lot message plus MaterialType, LotStatus, TemperatureRange, and HazardClass enums.
nodes.languageNotes: Proto3 service with 5 RPCs and request/response message pairs; enums follow the FOO_UNSPECIFIED=0 convention. Realized by apps/api/internal/handler/lot_service.go and consumed by the web Connect-ES client.

nodes.name: qc.proto
nodes.summary: Connect RPC schema for the QCService, the core AI-assisted plus human quality-control domain: presigned image upload/view URLs, QC job creation, AI inference results, and supervisor review/retry. Defines QCJob, QCFinding, QCResult messages and QCJobStatus, QCRecommendation, SupervisorDecision enums.
nodes.languageNotes: Largest of the three services with 7 RPCs spanning object-storage URL minting, async AI jobs, and human-in-the-loop review. Realized by apps/api/internal/handler/qc_service.go and consumed by the web Connect-ES client.

nodes.name: warehouse.proto
nodes.summary: Connect RPC schema for the WarehouseService handling cold-chain slot assignment and capacity: list locations, recommend slots, assign slots, and query assignments. Defines WarehouseLocation, WarehouseAssignment, SlotRecommendation messages and LocationStatus, AssignmentStatus enums.
nodes.languageNotes: Proto3 service with 4 RPCs; WarehouseLocation carries temperature_min/max and drum_compatibility for cold-chain matching. Realized by apps/api/internal/handler/warehouse_service.go and consumed by the web Connect-ES client.

nodes.name: LotService.CreateLot
nodes.summary: RPC that creates a new lot from supplier/material details and storage requirements, with an idempotency key to guard against duplicate intake.

nodes.name: LotService.GetLot
nodes.summary: RPC that fetches a single lot by its identifier.

nodes.name: LotService.ListLots
nodes.summary: RPC that lists lots with pagination and optional status/material-type filtering.

nodes.name: LotService.UpdateLotStatus
nodes.summary: RPC that transitions a lot to a new LotStatus with a reason, driving the intake-to-production workflow.

nodes.name: LotService.GetLotTimeline
nodes.summary: RPC that returns the audit timeline of actions taken on a lot, including actor and role per entry.

nodes.name: QCService.CreateQCUploadUrl
nodes.summary: RPC that mints a presigned object-storage upload URL for a QC image tied to a lot.

nodes.name: QCService.CreateQCViewUrl
nodes.summary: RPC that mints a short-lived presigned URL for viewing a stored QC image by object key.

nodes.name: QCService.CreateQCJob
nodes.summary: RPC that enqueues an AI QC inference job for an uploaded image, returning the queued QCJob.

nodes.name: QCService.GetQCJob
nodes.summary: RPC that retrieves a QC job and its current status for a given lot.

nodes.name: QCService.GetQCResult
nodes.summary: RPC that returns the AI inference result for a QC job, including recommendation, confidence, and findings.

nodes.name: QCService.ReviewQC
nodes.summary: RPC for human supervisor review of an AI QC result, recording an approve/reject/recheck decision and optionally rerunning AI.

nodes.name: QCService.RetryQCJob
nodes.summary: RPC that retries a failed QC job, re-queuing it for AI inference with a stated reason.

nodes.name: WarehouseService.ListLocations
nodes.summary: RPC that lists warehouse locations with optional zone and status filtering.

nodes.name: WarehouseService.RecommendSlot
nodes.summary: RPC that recommends scored cold-chain storage slots for a lot based on temperature and compatibility constraints.

nodes.name: WarehouseService.AssignSlot
nodes.summary: RPC that assigns a lot to a specific warehouse location, returning the created WarehouseAssignment.

nodes.name: WarehouseService.GetWarehouseAssignments
nodes.summary: RPC that queries warehouse assignments filtered by lot or location, with pagination.

layers.description: Go Connect-RPC backend service handlers, JWT/RBAC/audit/idempotency middleware, Keycloak auth, MinIO storage, and OpenTelemetry wiring for lot/QC/warehouse/admin/audit/dashboard/dispatch APIs.

layers.description: Transactional outbox publisher and the SSE/events hub that bridge TiDB writes to NATS JetStream and stream live updates to the web clients.

layers.name: AI Inference Worker

layers.name: Warehouse Intelligence Service
layers.description: Python FastAPI service for smart slotting, hazard segregation, and cold-chain monitoring of warehouse assignments.

layers.name: API Contracts & Schema
layers.description: Protobuf service and message definitions (lot, qc, warehouse, admin, audit, dashboard, dispatch) plus buf codegen config that define the cross-service contract.

layers.description: Helm charts, Kubernetes manifests, Keycloak realm, SST IaC, OTel collector config, Dockerfiles, docker-compose stack, GitHub Actions CI, and root build/workspace config.

layers.name: Testing & Verification
layers.description: k6 load and chaos-test harness plus end-to-end verification shell scripts that exercise the running system.

tour.title: Project Overview & Specs
tour.description: Start here to learn what SimaOps AI is: a single auditable platform that carries Sima Arome's natural-extracts through intake, AI-assisted QC, human approval, cold-chain warehousing, and dispatch. The README sketches the SvelteKit BFF to Go API to TiDB/MinIO/NATS to Python AI-worker pipeline, while docs/architecture.md and the authoritative design spec lock in service boundaries, the event-driven model, and the tech stack. Read these first so every later step has a place in the bigger picture.

tour.title: The API Contracts (Connect RPC)
tour.description: Before touching any implementation, learn the shared language between services. These Protobuf files define the six Connect RPC services that drive the whole pipeline: LotService governs the intake lifecycle, QCService covers presigned image upload plus AI and human review, WarehouseService handles cold-chain slot assignment, and DispatchService closes out the final stage. Every Go handler, web client, and event payload is generated from or validated against these contracts.
tour.languageLesson: Protobuf field numbers are permanent: never reuse a deleted number, and add only optional fields to stay backward compatible. Connect RPC serves these same service definitions over both gRPC and plain HTTP/JSON, which is how the browser BFF can call them directly.

tour.title: API Entry Point & Handler Wiring
tour.description: main.go is the runtime spine of the backend and the highest fan-out node in the codebase: it loads config, opens the DB pool, initializes OpenTelemetry, builds the events hub and NATS subscriber, then wraps JWT/RBAC/audit/idempotency middleware around the Connect and SSE handlers. connect.go is where each proto service from Step 2 becomes a concrete handler mounted on the HTTP mux. Together they show how a request enters the system and finds its service.
tour.languageLesson: In Go, the constructor-injection pattern seen here (passing the DB, hub, and clients into each handler struct) keeps dependencies explicit and makes handlers easy to test without globals.

tour.title: Lot Intake & QC Handlers
tour.description: This is the heart of the domain flow. The lot handler implements the intake lifecycle with transactional writes and outbox event emission, realizing the LotService contract from Step 2. The QC handler issues presigned MinIO upload/view URLs, enqueues AI inference jobs, records supervisor decisions, and — critically — triggers warehouse auto-assignment when a lot is approved. Watch how a single approval cascades forward into the next stage.

tour.title: Warehouse & Dispatch Handlers
tour.description: Continuing the pipeline from QC approval, the warehouse handler performs transactional capacity reservation and the auto-assignment kicked off in Step 4, choosing a cold-chain slot for the lot. The dispatch handler then closes the loop, creating dispatches and transitioning their status inside DB transactions with outbox events. Reading these two completes the intake to QC to warehouse to dispatch journey at the API layer.

tour.title: Data Access & Persistence
tour.description: The handlers in Steps 4-5 never write raw SQL inline; they call into this sqlc-generated layer. db.go defines the DBTX interface and the WithTx helper that powers every transactional write you just saw, while querier.go enumerates every available query method. The initial schema migration defines the lots table (and its siblings) that these queries read and write, anchoring the domain model in TiDB.
tour.languageLesson: sqlc generates type-safe Go directly from .sql query files, so a typo in a column name fails at compile time rather than at runtime. The Querier interface it emits also makes the data layer trivial to mock in handler tests.

tour.title: Realtime: The Outbox Publisher
tour.description: Every mutation in Steps 4-5 wrote an outbox event in the same transaction as the business data. This standalone Go service runs a Kubernetes leader-elected poll loop that claims PENDING outbox rows, publishes them to NATS JetStream with trace propagation, and marks them published or failed. On the API side, the NATS subscriber consumes those domain events back off the bus and forwards them into the in-memory events hub for fan-out.
tour.languageLesson: The transactional outbox pattern guarantees an event is published if and only if its database write commits, avoiding dual-write inconsistency. Leader election ensures exactly one publisher instance polls the table, so events are never double-emitted across replicas.

tour.title: Realtime: SSE Hub & Filtering
tour.description: Once an event reaches the subscriber, the hub fans it out to locally-connected clients, registering per-user connections with LRU eviction and supporting jittered draining for graceful shutdown. The filter computes allowed subject patterns per role and decides, per envelope, whether a subscriber may receive it based on role and ownership. The SSE handler ties it together: it registers the authenticated client, streams filtered frames, and kicks the client when its token expires.
tour.languageLesson: Server-Sent Events is a one-way HTTP streaming protocol: a single long-lived response where the server pushes text/event-stream frames. It is lighter than WebSockets when you only need server-to-client updates, and it reconnects automatically in the browser.

tour.title: AI Inference Worker
tour.description: This Python service is the consumer end of the QC pipeline from Step 4. It subscribes to NATS JetStream QC-image jobs, downloads the uploaded image from MinIO, runs OpenCV/YOLOv8 inference through a fuzzy-logic grader, writes QC results to the database, and publishes result and lifecycle events back through NATS — which then flow to clients via the hub from Step 8. It also exposes FastAPI health and metrics endpoints for observability.
tour.languageLesson: The worker uses Python asyncio to await NATS messages and I/O without blocking, so a single process can stream many jobs concurrently. NATS JetStream adds durable, at-least-once delivery on top of core NATS, so a worker restart does not drop in-flight inspection jobs.

tour.title: Warehouse Intelligence Service
tour.description: The warehouse auto-assignment from Step 5 does not compute slots itself — it delegates to this separate Python FastAPI service. The service exposes a slotting recommendation endpoint, a cold-chain status endpoint, and a health check, plus a background sensor-simulation loop. On the Go side, a thin HTTP client calls it to obtain ML-ranked slot recommendations for a lot, keeping the heavy optimization logic out of the core API.

tour.title: Web BFF: Auth & Reverse Proxy
tour.description: Now cross to the frontend. The SvelteKit server hook intercepts every request to resolve the session, refresh expiring tokens, manage auth cookies, and apply security headers. The auth core implements Keycloak OIDC with PKCE, signed auth-state, token exchange, and HttpOnly session cookies. The catch-all proxy then forwards Connect RPC and SSE traffic to the Go API, injecting the bearer token, enforcing same-origin, and rejecting SSRF-shaped paths.
tour.languageLesson: This is the Backend-for-Frontend (BFF) auth pattern: tokens live only in HttpOnly cookies on the server side and are never exposed to browser JavaScript, which neutralizes token-theft via XSS. PKCE protects the OIDC authorization-code exchange for public clients without a static secret.

tour.title: Web Realtime Client & App Shell
tour.description: This is the browser end of the realtime pipeline that began with the outbox in Step 7. The realtime store opens an EventSource to the BFF /events bridge and fans server events into TanStack Query cache invalidation, toasts, and live nav badges, handling heartbeat and exponential-backoff reconnect. The root layout wires this store into the app shell so the entire UI updates live as lots move through QC, warehousing, and dispatch.
tour.languageLesson: The .svelte.ts extension marks a module that uses Svelte 5 runes ($state, $derived) for reactivity outside a component, letting a plain TypeScript store drive UI updates. EventSource is the browser's native SSE client and pairs directly with the server stream from Step 8.

tour.title: Key Pages: Dashboard & QC Review
tour.description: These pages are where operators actually work, consuming everything built so far. The dashboard composes KPI and status cards into the operations overview and updates live from the realtime store of Step 12. The QC review screen polls the latest AI result for a lot, renders the annotated inspection image (produced by the worker in Step 9) alongside findings, and lets a supervisor approve, reject, or recheck — the very decision that cascades into warehouse assignment back in Step 5.

tour.title: Infrastructure & Deployment
tour.description: Finally, see how all five services ship. The API Dockerfile uses a multi-stage build to compile a static binary and ship it in a minimal distroless image, and the other services follow the same shape. docker-compose.yml brings up the full local stack — TiDB, MinIO, NATS, Keycloak, and tracing — that every prior step depends on, while the production workflow deploys to Oracle Kubernetes Engine via Helm on each release. This closes the loop from source to running cluster.
tour.languageLesson: Multi-stage Docker builds keep build-time tools (the Go compiler) out of the final image, and a distroless/nonroot base shrinks the attack surface to almost nothing. In CI YAML, an `on: release` trigger scopes the deploy to a protected environment so production ships only on a published release tag.
